name: Saigon's Restaurant
tagline: Honest Southern Vietnamese cooking on Waialae — the yellow curry wins people over and the pho is among the better bowls on the island.
mustOrder: vermicelli bowl — a solid everyday lunch
talkStory: Run by the Nguyen family on Waialae Ave since the early 2000s, serving Southern Vietnamese home cooking to the Kaimuki neighborhood. The pho broth simmers for 12+ hours and the yellow curry — a Southern Vietnamese specialty less common in Hawaii — is the sleeper hit that converts first-timers into regulars. While The Pig and the Lady gets the press for Vietnamese-fusion, Saigon's is where local Vietnamese families actually eat on their days off. No frills, honest prices, generous portions.
